article_title: Updates to liquor licensing process for private corporations

briefs.title: BC Liquor Licensing Now Includes Directors and Officers in Tied House Rules
briefs.summary: BC's liquor regulator updated its rules so that a company's directors and officers are now counted when deciding if a 'tied house' conflict exists — meaning a business relationship that unfairly favours one supplier's products. A new online Legal Entity Review process also lets the regulator request corporate documents through the licensing portal.
briefs.action_required: Log in to the BC liquor and cannabis licensing portal to report any tied house associations under 'Permanent Change to a Licensee'.
briefs.article_title: Updates to liquor licensing process for private corporations
